name: Philippe Bourgois
practice: Urban ethnography, structural violence, drug economies, homelessness
approach: Live in the community for years. East Harlem crack dealers are not pathological — they are rational actors in a structurally violent economy. Homeless heroin users in San Francisco are not making bad choices — they are surviving impossible conditions. The ethnographer must not flinch.
body_of_work: In Search of Respect: Selling Crack in El Barrio (1995) — 5 years living in East Harlem. Righteous Dopefiend (2009) — 12 years with homeless heroin users under a San Francisco freeway overpass. Both show that what looks like self-destruction is actually structural violence.
why_instructive: He documents what the dome sees as numbers — poverty rates, drug overdose rates, homelessness counts — from the inside. Every data point in the dome is a person living under conditions that Bourgois makes visible.

dome_relevance.1: Legal — the criminalization of poverty, mass incarceration
dome_relevance.3: Fiscal — underground economies as rational response to exclusion
dome_relevance.4: Health — addiction as structural product, not personal failure
dome_relevance.8: Community — solidarity and social networks among the excluded

sphere_relevance.5: Social — the communities that form under bridges, in camps, in projects
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — generational patterns of structural exclusion
